title: brodiaea douglasii var. howellii
intro: Brodiaea douglasii var. howellii, also known as Howell's brodiaea, is a plant species native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. It is a type of flowering plant in the asparagus family (Asparagaceae) that produces white or pinkish flowers. The medicinal properties of Howell's brodiaea are attributed to its roots, which contain alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures.
